description: 10-turn conversation mixing important facts with casual chat. Tests selective storage — the agent must NOT store everything, only decision-relevant information.

initial_memories.content: User is building a mobile app for restaurant reservations. Uses React Native.

turns.user_message: Hey, good morning! How's it going?
turns.response_context: Casual greeting. No memory ops needed.

turns.user_message: I've been thinking about push notifications. We need to implement them for reservation confirmations and reminders.
Yeah, sounds good. Let me grab some coffee first though, brb.
Ok I'm back. So for push notifications, should we use Firebase Cloud Messaging or Apple Push Notification service directly?
turns.response_context: Question needing project context for advice. Retrieve, don't store.

turns.user_message: Let's go with Firebase Cloud Messaging. It handles both iOS and Android which is what we need for React Native.
Oh man, did you see the game last night? Crazy ending.
Anyway, back to work. We also need to set up a staging environment. Our backend is running on Heroku right now but we're thinking about moving to AWS.
Can you remind me what our current tech stack looks like?

expected_ops.must_recall_correctly: React Native, Firebase Cloud Messaging, Heroku backend considering AWS

turns.response_context: Recall request. Should retrieve and summarize.

turns.user_message: Great, that's right. Oh, one more thing — our database is MongoDB Atlas with about 50,000 restaurant listings.
Alright, thanks for the help today. Talk later!

correct_epistemic_behavior: Do NOT store casual greetings, coffee breaks, sports chat, or farewells
Store technology decisions (FCM) and infrastructure facts (Heroku, MongoDB)
Retrieve when user asks for recall
Retrieve project context when giving architectural advice
Final memory count should be small (4-6 new memories + 1 initial)
incorrect_behaviors: Storing 'good morning', 'brb', 'game last night', or 'talk later' as memories
Storing every single message regardless of content
Not storing the FCM decision or MongoDB information
Failing to retrieve when user asks 'remind me what our tech stack looks like'
Having more than 8 total memories at the end (sign of over-storing)

key_claims.claim: The system stores casual chat messages
The system retrieves when asked for recall
The final memory is compact and relevant
